**DR Congo (COD):** In FIBA's team list of 13 October, 2020 national champions [ASB Mazembe](/wiki/ASB_Mazembe "ASB Mazembe") was announced to be the Congolese representative. However, the Fédération de basketball du Congo (FEBACO) eventually decided to enter 2021 champions [Espoir Fukash](/wiki/BC_Espoir_Fukash "BC Espoir Fukash").[[7]](#cite_note-8)

**Morocco (MOR):** [AS Salé](/wiki/AS_Sal%C3%A9_(basketball) "AS Salé (basketball)") was initially drawn into the second qualifying round, which it withdrew from. Later, the team was awarded the void spot left after the exclusion of the [Rivers Hoopers](/wiki/Rivers_Hoopers "Rivers Hoopers") of Nigeria.

**Nigeria (NGR):** Nigeria's [Rivers Hoopers](/wiki/Rivers_Hoopers "Rivers Hoopers") were excluded from the competition as [FIBA Africa](/wiki/FIBA_Africa "FIBA Africa") ruled the championship it won was not valid because it was not hosted by the [Nigeria Basketball Federation](/wiki/Nigeria_Basketball_Federation "Nigeria Basketball Federation").[[8]](#cite_note-9).

**Rwanda (RWA):** The national champions from Rwanda were given direct qualification, as the country will host the semi-finals and finals. Morocco's representative, [AS Salé](/wiki/AS_Sal%C3%A9_(basketball) "AS Salé (basketball)"), has been dropped from the six countries which qualified directly and will enter the Elite 16 of the qualifying tournament.[[9]](#cite_note-10)

**Zimbabwe (ZIM):** The representative team from Zimbabwe, [Mercenaries](/wiki/Mercenaries_(basketball) "Mercenaries (basketball)") pulled out before its games in Group F started.[[10]](#cite_note-teamsbattle-11)

The **2022 BAL season**, also known as **BAL Season 2**, was the second season of the [Basketball Africa League](/wiki/Basketball_Africa_League "Basketball Africa League") (BAL).[[1]](#cite_note-1) The season began on 2 March 2022 and ended with the [Finals](/wiki/2022_BAL_Finals "2022 BAL Finals") on 28 May 2022.[[2]](#cite_note-seasonbal-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3) The group phase was played in the [Dakar Arena](/wiki/Dakar_Arena "Dakar Arena") in [Dakar](/wiki/Dakar "Dakar") and the [Hassan Moustafa Sports Hall](/wiki/Hassan_Moustafa_Sports_Hall "Hassan Moustafa Sports Hall") in [Cairo](/wiki/Cairo "Cairo"). The playoffs and finals were played in the [BK Arena](/wiki/BK_Arena "BK Arena")[[A]](#cite_note-bkarena-5) in [Kigali](/wiki/Kigali "Kigali") for a second year in a row.

Tunisian club [US Monastir](/wiki/US_Monastir_(basketball) "US Monastir (basketball)") won the finals to win its first-ever BAL title, after defeating Angolan club [Petro de Luanda](/wiki/Petro_de_Luanda_(basketball) "Petro de Luanda (basketball)") in the [2022 BAL Finals](/wiki/2022_BAL_Finals "2022 BAL Finals").[[5]](#cite_note-monastirchampions-6) As winners, Monastir qualified for the [2023 FIBA Intercontinental Cup](/wiki/2023_FIBA_Intercontinental_Cup "2023 FIBA Intercontinental Cup").

## Format

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2022_BAL_season&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Format")]

On December 9, 2021, the BAL announced a new format for the season, with an expansion of the number of total games to 38. The twelve qualified teams are divided over two conference of six teams, in which all teams play the other teams once. The top eight teams from both conferences advance to the playoffs, which remains a single-elimination tournament.[[2]](#cite_note-seasonbal-2)

## Team allocation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2022_BAL_season&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Team allocation")]

On 13 October 2021, [FIBA](/wiki/FIBA "FIBA") announced the 26 teams from 26 countries which participate in the qualification round.[[6]](#cite_note-start-7) Eventually, 8 teams withdrew from the qualifications.

* 1st, 2nd, etc.: Place in the domestic competition

* CW: Cup winner

## Teams

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2022_BAL_season&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Teams")]

The twelve teams for the inaugural BAL season had to qualify in their domestic competitions to be able to play in the league, similar to other [FIBA](/wiki/FIBA "FIBA")-organised competitions. Six teams qualify directly as domestic champions; six winners of the qualifying tournaments qualify as well.

### Qualified teams

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2022_BAL_season&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Qualified teams")]

On 22 May 2020, [Petro de Luanda](/wiki/Atl%C3%A9tico_Petr%C3%B3leos_de_Luanda_(basketball) "Atlético Petróleos de Luanda (basketball)") was announced as the first qualified team for the 2021 BAL season. The Angolan [FAB](/wiki/Angolan_Basketball_Federation "Angolan Basketball Federation") had abandoned the season due to the [COVID-19 pandemic](/wiki/COVID-19_pandemic "COVID-19 pandemic") and awarded the team the place without naming it as champions.[[11]](#cite_note-petro-12) Four teams made their debut in the competition. On January 14, 2022, [FIBA Africa](/wiki/FIBA_Africa "FIBA Africa") decided to exclude the [Rivers Hoopers](/wiki/Rivers_Hoopers "Rivers Hoopers") as it stated that the [Nigeria Basketball Federation](/wiki/Nigeria_Basketball_Federation "Nigeria Basketball Federation") failed to organise a national championship.[[12]](#cite_note-13) The BAL chose to give [AS Salé](/wiki/AS_Sal%C3%A9_(basketball) "AS Salé (basketball)") direct qualification to replace the Nigerian team. [REG](/wiki/REG_BBC "REG BBC") qualified directly for the regular season as Rwanda hosted the playoffs and finals.

The twelve teams were confirmed by the BAL on 9 December 2021.[[2]](#cite_note-seasonbal-2)

[Cape Town Tigers](/wiki/Cape_Town_Tigers "Cape Town Tigers") ([South Africa](/wiki/South_Africa "South Africa")), [Cobra Sport](/wiki/Cobra_Sport "Cobra Sport") ([South Sudan](/wiki/South_Sudan "South Sudan")), [SLAC](/wiki/SLAC_(basketball_club) "SLAC (basketball club)") ([Guinea](/wiki/Guinea "Guinea")) and [Espoir Fukash](/wiki/BC_Espoir_Fukash "BC Espoir Fukash") ([DR Congo](/wiki/DR_Congo "DR Congo")) all were the first teams from their countries to play in the BAL.

## Qualifying tournaments

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2022_BAL_season&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Qualifying tournaments")]

Main article: [2022 BAL qualification](/wiki/2022_BAL_qualification "2022 BAL qualification")

The qualifying tournaments started on 21 October 2021 and ended on 16 December 2021, with 23 teams from 23 countries participating. Six teams qualified for the group phase.

## Group phase

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2022_BAL_season&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Group phase")]

The group phase began on 5 March 2022 in the [Dakar Arena](/wiki/Dakar_Arena "Dakar Arena") in [Dakar](/wiki/Dakar "Dakar"), where 15 games will be played. From 9 April to 19 April 2022, the group phase continued in [Hassan Moustafa Sports Hall](/wiki/Hassan_Moustafa_Sports_Hall "Hassan Moustafa Sports Hall") in [Giza](/wiki/Giza "Giza"). The four best teams of each conference advanced to the [playoffs](#playoffs).

## Playoffs

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2022_BAL_season&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Playoffs")]

Main article: [2022 BAL Playoffs](/wiki/2022_BAL_Playoffs "2022 BAL Playoffs")

The playoffs began on 21 May and ended on 28 May with the [Finals](/wiki/2022_BAL_Finals "2022 BAL Finals"). All games were played in a single-elimination format, and the entire tournament was played at the [Kigali Arena](/wiki/Kigali_Arena "Kigali Arena") in [Kigali](/wiki/Kigali "Kigali").

## Awards

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2022_BAL_season&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Awards")]
